testcase.name: 01 version command reports semver
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf version | grep -Eq "^[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+"

testcase.name: 02 top-level help lists check command
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf help | grep -q "check parse and validate policy"

testcase.name: 03 rules command help is implemented
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf help rules | grep -q "Status: implemented"

testcase.name: 04 basic policy validates as JSON
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf check --json fixtures/policies/basic.lpf | grep -q "\"valid\":true"

testcase.name: 05 invalid syntax policy is rejected
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c ! lpf check fixtures/policies/invalid-syntax.lpf fixtures/policies/invalid-syntax.lpf:1:13: error: expected default action `deny` or `pass` fixtures/policies/invalid-syntax.lpf:3:7: error: expected table name like `<trusted>` fixtures/policies/invalid-syntax.lpf:5:6: error: invalid rule: unexpected token `sideways`

testcase.name: 06 formatted policy passes fmt check
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf fmt --check fixtures/policies/basic.lpf | grep -q "is formatted"

testcase.name: 07 messy policy formatter emits normalized policy
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf fmt fixtures/policies/messy.lpf | grep -q "set default deny"

testcase.name: 08 semantic plan includes checksum
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf plan --json fixtures/policies/basic.lpf | grep -q "\"checksum\":\"md5:"

testcase.name: 09 semantic plan preserves default deny
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf plan --json fixtures/policies/basic.lpf | grep -q "\"default_action\":\"deny\""

testcase.name: 10 nftables renderer emits filter table
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf rules show fixtures/policies/basic.lpf | grep -q "table inet lpf_filter"

testcase.name: 11 unchanged nftables fixture has no diff
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf rules diff --observed fixtures/nftables/basic.nft fixtures/policies/basic.lpf | grep -q "no changes"

testcase.name: 12 changed nftables fixture reports changes
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf rules diff --observed fixtures/nftables-diff/changed-rule.diff fixtures/policies/basic.lpf | grep -q "changes required"

testcase.name: 13 tc backend renders qdisc plan
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf plan --backend tc fixtures/policies/queue-route.lpf | grep -q "tc qdisc add"

testcase.name: 14 routing backend renders fwmark rule
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf plan --backend routing fixtures/policies/queue-route.lpf | grep -q "ip rule add fwmark"

testcase.name: 15 nat and rdr policy validates
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf check --json fixtures/policies/nat-rdr.lpf | grep -q "\"valid\":true"

testcase.name: 16 nat and rdr rules include dnat
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf rules show fixtures/policies/nat-rdr.lpf | grep -q "dnat ip to 10.0.0.5:8080"

testcase.name: 17 explain reports trusted ssh pass
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf explain in eth0 from 10.0.0.5 to 192.168.1.1 proto tcp port 22 fixtures/policies/exhaustive.lpf | grep -q "Decision: pass"

testcase.name: 18 explain reports untrusted ssh block
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf explain in eth0 from 8.8.8.8 to 10.0.0.10 proto tcp port 22 fixtures/policies/exhaustive.lpf | grep -q "Decision: block"

testcase.name: 19 exhaustive policy assertions pass with junit
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf test --junit junit-lpf-policy.xml fixtures/tests/exhaustive.lpf.test | grep -q "OK:"

testcase.name: 20 generated man pages are current
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf man check --dir man/generated | grep -q "checked"

testcase.name: 21 openai tool schemas include check
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf tools --format openai | grep -q "\"name\":\"check\""

testcase.name: 22 jsonschema tool schemas include lpf-check
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf tools --format jsonschema | grep -q "lpf-check"

testcase.name: 23 system prompt mentions firewall automation
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf tools --format system-prompt | grep -q "firewall automation agent"

testcase.name: 24 bash completion includes command helper
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf completion bash | grep -q "_lpf_commands"

testcase.name: 25 zsh completion includes compdef
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf completion zsh | grep -q "#compdef lpf"

testcase.name: 26 fish completion includes lpf commands
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf completion fish | grep -q "complete -c lpf"

testcase.name: 27 sysctl diff emits JSON status
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf diff --backend sysctl --json fixtures/policies/basic.lpf | grep -q "\"backend\":\"sysctl\""

testcase.name: 28 apply dry-run reports plan checksum
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c lpf apply --dry-run fixtures/policies/basic.lpf | grep -q "dry-run: plan checksum"

testcase.name: 29 observed tc diff sees no changes
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c tmp="$(mktemp)"; lpf plan --backend tc fixtures/policies/queue-route.lpf >"$tmp"; lpf diff --backend tc --observed "$tmp" fixtures/policies/queue-route.lpf | grep -q "no changes"; rm -f "$tmp"

testcase.name: 30 qemu boots host kernel smoke vm
testcase.system-out: command: bash -c ci/semaphore/qemu-smoke.sh reports/qemu-smoke.log qemu-system-x86_64 not found
